rows.locks: Godot adapter IR maps into Ouroforge-native candidate scenes, entities, assets, inputs, oracle records, and an honest fidelity report without claiming any mapped unit is ported.
Lossy imports, unsupported features, script callbacks, physics-sensitive collider facts, and behavioral units remain visible Yellow/Red gaps and cannot be laundered into a clean Green report.
The mapping validator rejects claimed_ported_units and oracle-missing records with port permission, so ungated or auto-translated port claims fail until later Ouroforge-native oracle evidence passes.
The mapping artifact state-hash is stable for identical canonical IR mappings, changes when source data changes, and validation rejects stale hashes after artifact tampering.

Coverage v79 records the one-way on-ramp, source-project/open-text legal boundary, clean-room re-derivation hand-off, Rust data-plane ownership, no Studio trusted writes, and #1/#23 open governance anchors.

boundary: Scenario Coverage v79 is a Rust data-plane regression suite for Era O M90 IR to Ouroforge mapping and fidelity classification. It locks source-project/open-text adapter IR mapping into Ouroforge-native candidate skeleton artifacts, no foreign runtime bridge, no auto-port or ported claim without a passing oracle, honest Yellow/Red fidelity gaps for lossy imports, deterministic state-hash behavior including stale-hash rejection, and Rust-owned artifact truth. Elixir/Phoenix Studio is not touched and has no trusted write or artifact semantics authority. #1 and #23 remain open.
